facts.fact: The contract title is 'Contract with Approved Entity Pursuant to Sections 1860D-1 through 1860D-43 of the Social Security Act for the Operation of a Voluntary Medicare Prescription Drug Plan'.
The contract is between the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (hereinafter referred to as 'CMS') and the PDP Sponsor.
CMS is identified as 'Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services'.
The counterparty to CMS is a Prescription Drug Plan Sponsor (hereinafter referred to as 'PDP Sponsor').
The PDP Sponsor has been determined eligible to operate a Voluntary Medicare Prescription Drug Plan by the Administrator of CMS under 42 CFR § 423.503.
The contract is entered into for the purposes of §§ 1860D-1 through 1860D-43 of the Social Security Act, with the exception of §§ 1860D-22(a) and 1860D-31.
The Social Security Act is hereinafter referred to as 'the Act'.
PDP Sponsor agrees to operate one or more Medicare Voluntary Prescription Drug Plans (hereinafter referred to as a 'PDP').
PDP Sponsor agrees to operate the PDP as described in its application and related materials submitted to CMS for Medicare approval.
PDP Sponsor agrees to operate the PDP in compliance with all attestations contained in its application submitted to CMS.
The contract incorporates in its entirety the 2025 Solicitation for Applications for Medicare Prescription Drug Plan Contracts.
The 2025 Solicitation for Applications for Medicare Prescription Drug Plan Contracts was released on January 9, 2024.
The 2025 Solicitation for Applications for Medicare Prescription Drug Plan Contracts is hereinafter collectively referred to as 'the contract'.
PDP Sponsor agrees to operate in accordance with §§ 1860D-1 through 1860D-43 of the Act, with the exception of §§ 1860D-22(a) and 1860D-31.
PDP Sponsor agrees to operate in accordance with the regulations at 42 CFR Part 423, with the exception of Subparts Q, R, and S.
PDP Sponsor agrees to operate in accordance with the Medicare Prescription Drug Benefit Manual.
PDP Sponsor agrees to operate in accordance with the Medicare Communications and Marketing Guidelines.
PDP Sponsor agrees to operate in accordance with the Final CY 2025 Part D Redesign Program Instructions.
PDP Sponsor agrees to operate in accordance with the Medicare Part D Manufacturer Discount Program Final Guidance.
PDP Sponsor agrees to operate in accordance with the Medicare Prescription Payment Plan Final Guidance.
PDP Sponsor agrees to operate in accordance with CMS Participant Guides.
PDP Sponsor agrees to operate in accordance with Health Plan Management System memos.
PDP Sponsor agrees to operate in accordance with the Rate Announcement.
PDP Sponsor agrees to operate in accordance with CMS trainings.
The contract is deemed to incorporate any changes required by statute to be implemented during the term of the contract, except as provided in Article I paragraph (C).
The contract is deemed to incorporate any regulations implementing or interpreting statutory provisions required during the contract term, except as provided in Article I paragraph (C).
CMS agrees to perform its obligations to PDP Sponsor consistent with §§ 1860D-1 through 1860D-43 of the Act, with the exception of §§ 1860D-22(a) and 1860D-31.
CMS agrees to perform its obligations to PDP Sponsor consistent with the regulations at 42 CFR Part 423, with the exception of Subparts Q, R, and S.
CMS agrees to perform its obligations consistent with the Medicare Prescription Drug Benefit Manual.
CMS agrees to perform its obligations consistent with the Medicare Communications and Marketing Guidelines.
CMS agrees to perform its obligations consistent with the Final CY 2025 Part D Redesign Program Instructions.
CMS agrees to perform its obligations consistent with the Medicare Part D Manufacturer Discount Program Final Guidance.
CMS agrees to perform its obligations consistent with the Medicare Prescription Payment Plan Final Guidance.
CMS agrees to perform its obligations consistent with CMS Participant Guides.
CMS agrees to perform its obligations consistent with Health Plan Management System memos.
CMS agrees to perform its obligations consistent with the Rate Announcement.
CMS agrees to perform its obligations consistent with CMS trainings.
CMS agrees that it will not implement regulations under 42 CFR Part 423 that impose new, significant regulatory requirements on PDP Sponsor other than at the beginning of a calendar year.
The restriction on mid-year implementation of new significant regulatory requirements does not apply to new requirements mandated by statute.
The restriction on mid-year regulatory implementation is governed by 42 CFR § 423.516.
If PDP Sponsor had a contract with CMS for Contract Year 2024 under the contract ID number designated in this contract, this document is considered a renewal of the existing contract.
The terms of this contract supersede the terms of the 2024 contract.
The execution of this contract does not extinguish any pending obligations or actions that may have arisen under the 2024 or prior year contracts.
The execution of this contract does not interrupt any pending obligations or actions that may have arisen under the 2024 or prior year contracts.
This contract is in no way intended to supersede or modify 42 CFR Part 423.
Failure to reference a regulatory requirement in this contract does not affect the applicability of such requirements to PDP Sponsor.
Failure to reference a regulatory requirement in this contract does not affect the applicability of such requirements to CMS.
PDP Sponsor agrees to accept new enrollments as described in 42 CFR Part 423 Subpart B.
PDP Sponsor agrees to make enrollments effective as described in 42 CFR Part 423 Subpart B.
PDP Sponsor agrees to process voluntary disenrollments as described in 42 CFR Part 423 Subpart B.
PDP Sponsor agrees to limit involuntary disenrollments as described in 42 CFR Part 423 Subpart B.
PDP Sponsor agrees to comply with the prohibition on discrimination in beneficiary enrollment set forth in 42 CFR § 423.104(b).

PDP Sponsor shall accept enrollment applications from individuals who change their election during the open enrollment period for individuals enrolled in Medicare Advantage described in 42 CFR § 422.62(a)(3), as required by 42 CFR § 423.38(e).
PDP Sponsor agrees to provide basic prescription drug coverage as defined under 42 CFR § 423.100.
PDP Sponsor agrees to provide supplemental benefits as defined in 42 CFR § 423.100 and in accordance with Subpart C of 42 CFR Part 423, to the extent applicable.
PDP Sponsor agrees to provide Part D benefits as described in PDP Sponsor's bid(s) approved each year by CMS.
The Medicare Advantage Attestation of Benefit Plan is included in the contracting materials.
The Medicare Advantage Attestation of Benefit Plan is to be replaced each year upon renewal of the contract to reflect PDP Sponsor's approved bids for the succeeding contract year.
PDP Sponsor agrees to calculate beneficiary premiums in accordance with 42 CFR §§ 423.286 and 423.293.
PDP Sponsor agrees to collect beneficiary premiums in accordance with 42 CFR §§ 423.286 and 423.293.
PDP Sponsor agrees to make a reasonable effort to identify all amounts incorrectly collected from beneficiaries.
PDP Sponsor agrees to pay any amounts due in accordance with 42 CFR § 423.294.

PDP Sponsor agrees to maintain administrative and management capabilities sufficient to organize, implement, and control financial activities related to the delivery of Part D services as required by 42 CFR § 423.505(b)(25).

PDP Sponsor agrees to maintain administrative and management capabilities sufficient to organize, implement, and control communication activities related to the delivery of Part D services as required by 42 CFR § 423.505(b)(25).

PDP Sponsor agrees to maintain administrative and management capabilities sufficient to organize, implement, and control benefit administration activities related to the delivery of Part D services as required by 42 CFR § 423.505(b)(25).

PDP Sponsor agrees to maintain administrative and management capabilities sufficient to organize, implement, and control quality assurance activities related to the delivery of Part D services as required by 42 CFR § 423.505(b)(25).
PDP Sponsor agrees to provide applicable beneficiaries with manufacturer discounts on applicable drugs in the initial coverage phase of the Part D benefit in accordance with § 1860D-14C of the Act.

PDP Sponsor agrees to provide applicable beneficiaries with manufacturer discounts on applicable drugs in the catastrophic coverage phase of the Part D benefit in accordance with § 1860D-14C of the Act.
PDP Sponsor agrees to provide manufacturer discounts in accordance with the Medicare Part D Manufacturer Discount Program Final Guidance.
PDP Sponsor agrees to provide all Part D enrollees with the option to participate in the Medicare Prescription Payment Plan in accordance with § 1860D-2(b)(2)(E) of the Act.

PDP Sponsor agrees to provide all Part D enrollees with the option to participate in the Medicare Prescription Payment Plan in accordance with Medicare Prescription Payment Plan: Final Part One Guidance on Select Topics, Implementation of Section 1860D-2 of the Social Security Act for 2025, and Response to Relevant Comments.

PDP Sponsor agrees to provide all Part D enrollees with the option to participate in the Medicare Prescription Payment Plan in accordance with Medicare Prescription Payment Plan: Final Part Two Guidance on Select Topics, Implementation of Section 1860D-2 of the Social Security Act for 2025, and Response to Relevant Comments.
PDP Sponsor agrees to provide the information required in 42 CFR § 423.48.
PDP Sponsor acknowledges that CMS releases to the public summary reconciled Part D payment data after the reconciliation of Part D payments, as provided in 42 CFR § 423.505(o)(1).
PDP Sponsor acknowledges that CMS releases to the public Part D Medical Loss Ratio data for the contract year, as described at 42 CFR § 423.2490.
CMS's release of public data is consistent with 42 CFR Part 423 Subpart K.
PDP Sponsor agrees to disclose information to beneficiaries in the manner specified by CMS under 42 CFR § 423.128 and 42 CFR Part 423 Subpart V.
PDP Sponsor agrees to disclose information to beneficiaries in the form specified by CMS under 42 CFR § 423.128 and 42 CFR Part 423 Subpart V.
PDP Sponsor agrees to operate quality assurance programs in accordance with Subpart D of 42 CFR Part 423.
PDP Sponsor agrees to operate drug utilization management programs in accordance with Subpart D of 42 CFR Part 423.
PDP Sponsor agrees to operate drug management programs in accordance with Subpart D of 42 CFR Part 423.
PDP Sponsor agrees to operate medication therapy management programs in accordance with Subpart D of 42 CFR Part 423.
PDP Sponsor agrees to support electronic prescribing in accordance with Subpart D of 42 CFR Part 423.
PDP Sponsor agrees to address complaints received by CMS against the PDP Sponsor through the CMS complaint tracking system as required in 42 CFR § 423.505(b)(22).
PDP Sponsor agrees to resolve complaints received by CMS against the PDP Sponsor through the CMS complaint tracking system as required in 42 CFR § 423.505(b)(22).
PDP Sponsor agrees to maintain a Part D summary plan rating score of at least 3 stars as required by 42 CFR § 423.505(b)(26).
PDP Sponsor agrees to pass an essential operations test prior to the start of the benefit year.
The essential operations test requirement applies only to new sponsors that have not previously entered into a Part D contract with CMS.
The essential operations test requirement applies only where neither the new sponsor nor another subsidiary of the applicant's parent organization is offering Part D benefits during the current year.
The essential operations test requirement is governed by 42 CFR § 423.505(b)(27).
PDP Sponsor agrees to comply with all requirements in Subpart M of 42 CFR Part 423 governing coverage determinations.
PDP Sponsor agrees to comply with all requirements in Subpart M of 42 CFR Part 423 governing grievances and appeals.
PDP Sponsor agrees to comply with all requirements in Subpart M of 42 CFR Part 423 governing formulary exceptions.
PDP Sponsor agrees to comply with the applicable provisions of Subpart U of 42 CFR Part 423.
Payment under this contract is governed by the rules in Subpart G of 42 CFR Part 423.
PDP Sponsor agrees to comply with all applicable Federal laws and regulations pertaining to claims and debt collections.
PDP Sponsor agrees to comply with all applicable guidance and authorities pertaining to claims and debt collections.
In the event that the Government determines that PDP Sponsor has been overpaid, PDP Sponsor agrees to return those overpaid monies back to the Government.

If PDP Sponsor intends to participate in the Part D program for the next program year, PDP Sponsor agrees to submit the next year's bid by the applicable due date as provided in Subpart F of 42 CFR Part 423.
The next year's bid submission must include all required information on premiums, benefits, and cost-sharing.
The purpose of bid submission is to allow CMS and PDP Sponsor to conduct negotiations regarding the terms and conditions of the proposed bid and benefit plan renewal.
PDP Sponsor agrees to comply with State law to the extent that it is not preempted by Federal law, as described in Subpart I of 42 CFR Part 423.
A waiver granted to PDP Sponsor pursuant to 42 CFR § 423.410 shall be valid for three consecutive program years.

Expiration of the licensure waiver (under 42 CFR § 423.410) and failure to obtain a license from the relevant State may be the basis for CMS deleting from PDP Sponsor's service area those PDP Regions affected by the waiver expiration.
CMS may terminate PDP Sponsor's contract where the expiration of the waiver (under 42 CFR § 423.410) results in PDP Sponsor not being qualified to offer a PDP plan in any PDP Region.
CMS may non-renew PDP Sponsor's contract where the expiration of the waiver (under 42 CFR § 423.410) results in PDP Sponsor not being qualified to offer a PDP plan in any PDP Region.

A waiver granted to PDP Sponsor pursuant to 42 CFR § 423.415 shall be valid for the period that the Secretary of the Department of Health and Human Services determines is appropriate for timely processing of PDP Sponsor's license application by the State.
A waiver granted pursuant to 42 CFR § 423.415 shall be valid for no more than one year.
A waiver granted pursuant to 42 CFR § 423.415 begins on January 1 of the contract year for which CMS granted the waiver.
PDP Sponsor agrees to comply with coordination requirements with State Pharmacy Assistance Programs (SPAPs) as described in Subpart J of 42 CFR Part 423.
PDP Sponsor agrees to comply with coordination requirements with plans that provide other prescription drug coverage as described in Subpart J of 42 CFR Part 423.
PDP Sponsor agrees to comply with Medicare Secondary Payer procedures as described in 42 CFR § 423.462.
PDP Sponsor agrees to provide Part D benefits in the service area for which it has been approved by CMS.
PDP Sponsor agrees to utilize a pharmacy network approved by CMS that meets the requirements of 42 CFR § 423.120.
PDP Sponsor agrees to utilize a formulary approved by CMS that meets the requirements of 42 CFR § 423.120.
PDP Sponsor agrees to provide Part D benefits through out-of-network pharmacies according to 42 CFR § 423.124.

PDP Sponsor agrees to provide benefits by means of point of service systems to adjudicate prescription drug claims in a timely and efficient manner in compliance with CMS standards, according to 42 CFR § 423.505(b)(17).
The point of service systems requirement has an exception when necessary to provide access in underserved areas.
The point of service systems requirement has an exception for I/T/U pharmacies as defined in 42 CFR § 423.100.
The point of service systems requirement has an exception for long-term care pharmacies as defined in 42 CFR § 423.100.
PDP Sponsor agrees to contract with any pharmacy that meets PDP Sponsor's reasonable and relevant standard terms and conditions according to 42 CFR § 423.505(b)(18).
PDP Sponsor agrees to make standard contracts available on request in accordance with the timelines specified in 42 CFR § 423.505(b)(18).
PDP Sponsor agrees to adopt and implement an effective compliance program that applies to its Part D-related operations, consistent with 42 CFR § 423.504(b)(4)(vi).

PDP Sponsor agrees to provide notice to CMS, based on best knowledge, information, and belief, of any integrity items related to payments from governmental entities (Federal and State) for healthcare or prescription drug services that would have been reported as part of Table A of the Business Integrity section of the PDP application.
Integrity items subject to notice include investigations by a government agency (State or Federal) involving the PDP Sponsor or its subcontractors (excluding contracted network providers).
Integrity items subject to notice include investigations involving key management or executive staff of the PDP Sponsor or its subcontractors.
Integrity items subject to notice include investigations involving major shareholders (5% or more) of the PDP Sponsor or its subcontractors.

Integrity items subject to notice include legal actions or matters subject to arbitration by a government agency (State or Federal) on matters relating to payments from governmental entities for healthcare and/or prescription drug services.
PDP Sponsor shall keep the Government informed of when an integrity item is initiated.
PDP Sponsor shall keep the Government informed of when an integrity item is closed.
Notice must be provided of the details concerning any resolution and monetary payments related to integrity items.
Notice must be provided of any settlement agreements related to integrity items.
Notice must be provided of any corporate integrity agreements related to integrity items.

PDP Sponsor agrees to provide notice to CMS in the event PDP Sponsor or any of its subcontractors is criminally convicted for fraudulent activities involving the provision of health care or prescription drug services.

PDP Sponsor agrees to provide notice to CMS in the event PDP Sponsor or any of its subcontractors has a civil judgment entered against it for fraudulent activities involving the provision of health care or prescription drug services.

PDP Sponsor agrees to provide notice to CMS in the event PDP Sponsor or any of its subcontractors is sanctioned under any Federal program involving the provision of health care or prescription drug services.
PDP Sponsor agrees to participate in the administration of subsidies for low-income subsidy eligible individuals according to Subpart P of 42 CFR Part 423.
PDP Sponsor agrees to maintain the capacity to communicate with CMS electronically in accordance with CMS requirements.
PDP Sponsor agrees to afford its enrollees protection from liability for payment of fees that are the obligation of PDP Sponsor in accordance with 42 CFR § 423.505(g).
PDP Sponsor maintains ultimate responsibility for adhering to and fully complying with all terms and conditions of this contract with CMS, as governed by 42 CFR § 423.505(i).

PDP Sponsor shall ensure that any contracts or agreements with first tier entities performing functions on PDP Sponsor's behalf related to the operation of the Part D benefit are in compliance with 42 CFR § 423.505(i).

PDP Sponsor shall ensure that any contracts or agreements with downstream entities performing functions on PDP Sponsor's behalf related to the operation of the Part D benefit are in compliance with 42 CFR § 423.505(i).

PDP Sponsor shall ensure that any contracts or agreements with related entities performing functions on PDP Sponsor's behalf related to the operation of the Part D benefit are in compliance with 42 CFR § 423.505(i).

PDP Sponsor agrees not to employ or contract with an individual or entity who is excluded from participation in Medicare under Section 1128 of the Social Security Act for provision of any of the services listed in 42 CFR § 423.752(a)(6).

PDP Sponsor agrees not to employ or contract with an individual or entity who is excluded from participation in Medicare under Section 1128A of the Social Security Act for provision of any of the services listed in 42 CFR § 423.752(a)(6).

PDP Sponsor agrees not to employ or contract with an entity that employs or contracts with an individual excluded from participation in Medicare under Section 1128 or 1128A of the Social Security Act for provision of any of the services listed in 42 CFR § 423.752(a)(6).
PDP Sponsor must provide certifications in accordance with 42 CFR § 423.505(k).
PDP Sponsor agrees to payment of fees established by CMS for cost sharing of enrollment related costs in accordance with 42 CFR § 423.6.
The pharmacy reimbursement requirements in Article II-R are governed by 42 CFR §§ 423.505(b)(21) and 423.520.
If a PDP Sponsor uses a standard for reimbursement of pharmacies based on the cost of a drug, PDP Sponsor will update such standard not less frequently than once every 7 days.
The pharmacy reimbursement standard update must begin with an initial update on January 1 of each year.
The purpose of updating the pharmacy reimbursement standard is to accurately reflect the market price of the drug.

If the source for any prescription drug pricing standard is not publicly available, PDP Sponsor will disclose all individual drug prices to be updated to the applicable pharmacies in advance for their use for the reimbursement of claims.
PDP Sponsor will issue, mail, or otherwise transmit payment for claims submitted by pharmacies within 14 days of receipt of an electronically submitted claim.
PDP Sponsor will issue, mail, or otherwise transmit payment for claims submitted by pharmacies within 30 days of receipt of a claim submitted by non-electronic means.
The 14-day and 30-day pharmacy payment timeframes do not apply to pharmacies that dispense drugs by mail order only.
The 14-day and 30-day pharmacy payment timeframes do not apply to pharmacies that are located in a long-term care facility.
The 14-day and 30-day pharmacy payment timeframes do not apply to pharmacies that contract with a long-term care facility.
PDP Sponsor must ensure that a pharmacy located in a long-term care facility will have not less than 30 days to submit claims to PDP Sponsor for reimbursement.
PDP Sponsor must ensure that a pharmacy located in a long-term care facility will have not more than 90 days to submit claims to PDP Sponsor for reimbursement.
PDP Sponsor must ensure that a pharmacy having a contract with a long-term care facility will have not less than 30 days to submit claims to PDP Sponsor for reimbursement.
PDP Sponsor must ensure that a pharmacy having a contract with a long-term care facility will have not more than 90 days to submit claims to PDP Sponsor for reimbursement.
PDP Sponsor agrees to maintain records in accordance with 42 CFR §§ 423.505(b)(10) and 423.505(i)(2).
PDP Sponsor agrees to provide access to records in accordance with 42 CFR §§ 423.505(b)(10) and 423.505(i)(2).
PDP Sponsor agrees to submit information to CMS according to 42 CFR §§ 423.505(f) and 423.514.
PDP Sponsor agrees to submit information to CMS according to the applicable Final Medicare Part D Reporting Guidance.
PDP Sponsor agrees to submit to CMS each plan's formulary information, including any changes to its formularies.
PDP Sponsor grants to the Government a non-exclusive license to use all or any portion of the formulary for any purpose related to the administration of the Part D program.
The non-exclusive formulary license extends to any person or entity who might receive the formulary from the Government.
The non-exclusive formulary license permits publicly distributing the formulary information in any medium, including www.medicare.gov.
The non-exclusive formulary license permits displaying the formulary information in any medium.
The non-exclusive formulary license permits publishing the formulary information in any medium.
The non-exclusive formulary license permits reconfiguration of the formulary information in any medium.
The non-exclusive formulary license permits distribution by any electronic means.
The non-exclusive formulary license permits distribution by print or other means of distribution.
PDP Sponsor agrees to comply with the confidentiality requirements specified in 42 CFR § 423.136.
PDP Sponsor agrees to comply with the enrollee record accuracy requirements specified in 42 CFR § 423.136.
PDP Sponsor agrees to enter into a business associate agreement with the entity with which CMS has contracted to track Medicare beneficiaries' true out-of-pocket costs.
PDP Sponsor agrees to comply with applicable Federal laws and regulations designed to prevent fraud, waste, and abuse, including applicable provisions of Federal criminal law.
PDP Sponsor agrees to comply with the False Claims Act (31 U.S.C. §§ 3729 et seq.).
PDP Sponsor agrees to comply with the anti-kickback provision of § 1128B of the Act.
PDP Sponsor agrees to comply with applicable HIPAA rules at 45 CFR Part 160.
PDP Sponsor agrees to comply with applicable HIPAA rules at 45 CFR Part 162.
PDP Sponsor agrees to comply with applicable HIPAA rules at 45 CFR Part 164.
PDP Sponsor agrees to comply with all other applicable Federal statutes and regulations.
This contract is effective from the date of CMS's authorized representative's signature through December 31, 2025.
This contract shall be renewable for successive one-year periods after December 31, 2025, according to 42 CFR § 423.506.

PDP Sponsor will be determined qualified to renew its contract annually only if PDP Sponsor has not provided CMS with a notice of intention not to renew in accordance with Article VII of this contract, per 42 CFR § 423.507.
If PDP Sponsor and CMS cannot reach agreement on the bid under Subpart F of 42 CFR Part 423 and CMS declines to accept the bid pursuant to 42 CFR § 423.265(b)(4), no renewal takes place.
A failure to reach bid agreement is not subject to the appeals provisions in Subpart N of 42 CFR Part 423, in accordance with 42 CFR § 423.502(d)(2).

PDP Sponsor may elect not to renew its contract with CMS, effective at the end of the term of the contract, for any reason, as long as proper notice is provided according to required timeframes, per 42 CFR § 423.507(a)(1).
If PDP Sponsor does not intend to renew its contract, it must notify CMS in writing by the first Monday of June in the year in which the current contract period ends, per 42 CFR § 423.507(a)(2).
If PDP Sponsor does not intend to renew its contract, it must notify each Medicare enrollee at least 90 days before the date on which the nonrenewal is effective.

PDP Sponsor must provide enrollees, through the nonrenewal notice or outbound telephone calls, information on alternatives available for obtaining qualified prescription drug coverage within the PDP region.
The alternatives information provided to enrollees must include information about Medicare Advantage-Prescription Drug plans.
The alternatives information provided to enrollees must include information about Medicare cost plans offering a Part D plan.
The alternatives information provided to enrollees must include information about other PDPs.
PDP Sponsor must receive CMS approval of nonrenewal notices or scripts prior to their use.

If PDP Sponsor does not renew a contract, CMS cannot enter into a contract with PDP Sponsor for 2 years in the PDP region or regions served by the contract, unless CMS determines there are circumstances that warrant special consideration, per 42 CFR § 423.507(a)(3).

If PDP Sponsor does not renew a contract, CMS cannot enter into a contract with an organization whose covered persons (as defined in 42 CFR § 423.507(a)(4)) also served as covered persons for the nonrenewing PDP Sponsor for 2 years in the affected PDP region(s), unless CMS determines there are circumstances that warrant special consideration.

If a PDP Sponsor does not renew any of its plan benefit packages (PBPs) in a PDP region, CMS does not approve plan bids submitted by the organization in that PDP region for 2 years, unless CMS determines there are circumstances that warrant special consideration, per 42 CFR § 423.507(a)(3).
If PDP Sponsor does not renew a contract, it must ensure the timely transfer of any data or files in accordance with 42 CFR § 423.507(a)(5).
This contract may be modified at any time by written mutual consent of the parties.
This contract may be terminated at any time by written mutual consent of the parties.

If the PDP Sponsor submits a request to end the term of this contract after the deadline provided in 42 CFR § 423.507(a)(2)(i), CMS may mutually consent to the contract termination if the termination does not negatively affect the administration of the Medicare Part D Program.
If this contract is terminated by mutual consent, PDP Sponsor must provide notice to its Medicare enrollees in accordance with CMS's instructions.
If this contract is terminated by mutual consent, PDP Sponsor must provide notice to the general public in accordance with CMS's instructions.

If the contract is modified by mutual consent, PDP Sponsor must notify its Medicare enrollees of any changes that CMS determines are appropriate for notification, according to the process and timeframes specified by CMS, as set forth in 42 CFR § 423.508.
If a contract is terminated by mutual consent under Article VIII-A, PDP Sponsor must ensure the timely transfer of any data or files.

If a contract is terminated by mutual consent under Article VIII-A, CMS cannot enter into a new contract or service area expansion in the affected PDP region(s) with PDP Sponsor or an organization whose covered persons (as defined in 42 CFR § 423.508(f)) also served as covered persons for the terminating PDP Sponsor for a period of up to 2 years, unless CMS determines there are circumstances that warrant special consideration.
A PDP Sponsor that agrees to terminate its offering of PBPs in a PDP region agrees that it will not be eligible to apply to resume offering plans in that region for 2 years, per 42 CFR § 423.508(e).
CMS may terminate the contract in accordance with 42 CFR § 423.509.
PDP Sponsor may terminate the contract only in accordance with 42 CFR § 423.510.
PDP Sponsor shall be subject to sanctions consistent with Subpart O of 42 CFR Part 423.
PDP Sponsor shall be subject to civil money penalties consistent with Subpart O of 42 CFR Part 423.
CMS may determine that the PDP Sponsor is out of compliance with a Part D requirement and take compliance actions as described in 42 CFR § 423.505(n).
CMS may issue intermediate sanctions as defined in 42 CFR Part 423 Subpart O if PDP Sponsor is found out of compliance, per 42 CFR § 423.505(n).
Severability of the contract shall be in accordance with 42 CFR § 423.504(e).
Terms not otherwise defined in this contract shall have the meaning given to such terms in §§ 1860D-1 through 1860D-43 of the Act.
Terms not otherwise defined in this contract shall have the meaning given to such terms in 42 CFR Part 423.
PDP Sponsor agrees and attests that it has not altered in any way the terms of the PDP contract presented for signature by CMS.
Any alterations to the original text that PDP Sponsor may make to this contract shall not be binding on the parties.
PDP Sponsor agrees to include in this contract other terms and conditions in accordance with 42 CFR § 423.505(j).

Pursuant to § 13112 of the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009 (ARRA), PDP Sponsor agrees that as it implements, acquires, or upgrades its health information technology systems, it shall utilize, where available, health information technology systems and products that meet standards and implementation specifications adopted under § 3004 of the Public Health Service Act.
The health IT obligation references § 3004 of the Public Health Service Act as amended by § 13101 of the ARRA.
PDP Sponsor agrees to maintain a positive net worth (total assets exceed total liabilities) as required in 42 CFR § 423.505(b)(23).
Positive net worth is defined in this contract as total assets exceeding total liabilities.
PDP Sponsor agrees to develop a business continuity plan as required by 42 CFR § 423.505(p).
PDP Sponsor agrees to maintain a business continuity plan as required by 42 CFR § 423.505(p).
PDP Sponsor agrees to implement a business continuity plan as required by 42 CFR § 423.505(p).
PDP Sponsor agrees to comply with Title VI of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 and pertinent regulations at 45 CFR Part 80.
PDP Sponsor agrees to comply with § 504 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973 and pertinent regulations at 45 CFR Part 84.
PDP Sponsor agrees to comply with the Age Discrimination Act of 1975 and pertinent regulations at 45 CFR Part 91.
PDP Sponsor agrees to comply with the requirements relating to Nondiscrimination in Health Programs and Activities in 45 CFR Part 92.
PDP Sponsor must submit assurances that its health programs and activities are operated in compliance with nondiscrimination requirements, as required in 45 CFR § 92.5.
The final settlement process and payment will be handled in accordance with 42 CFR §§ 423.521 and 423.522.
Any appeals related to final settlement will be handled in accordance with 42 CFR §§ 423.521 and 423.522.
The contract is electronically signed on behalf of PDP Sponsor by the designated Contracting Official.
The contract is electronically signed on behalf of CMS by Vanessa S. Duran.
Vanessa S. Duran holds the title of Director, Medicare Drug Benefit and C & D Data Group, Center for Medicare.
The document is 13 pages in length.

Article I-C - Limitation on mid-year regulatory changes
Article I-E - Non-supersession of 42 CFR Part 423
Article II-C - Dissemination of Plan Information
Article II-F - Payment to PDP Sponsor
Article II-G - Bid Submission and Review
Article II-H - State Law and Licensure Requirements
Article II-I - Coordination with Other Prescription Drug Coverage
Article II-J - Service Area and Pharmacy Access
Article II-K - Effective Compliance Program/Program Integrity
Article II-O - Relationship with First Tier, Downstream, and Related Entities
Article II-P - Certification of Data That Determine Payment
Article II-R - PDP Sponsor Reimbursement to Pharmacies
Article III-A - Record Maintenance and Access
Article III-C - CMS License for Use of Plan Formulary
Article V - Requirements of Other Laws and Regulations
Article VI-B - Qualification to Renew a Contract
Article VII-C - Re-contracting restriction after nonrenewal
Article VII-D - Data transfer on nonrenewal
Article VIII-A - Contract Modification or Termination by Mutual Consent
Article VIII-C - Termination by PDP Sponsor
Article IX-B - Compliance Actions and Past Performance
Article XI-B - Alteration to Original Contract Terms
